summary: Set 047 Session 3 shipped the migrator phase of the v4 schema migration: the Python `python -m ai_router.migrate_v3_to_v4` CLI subcommand (dry-run + apply, idempotent, writes session-state.v3.bak.json alongside on apply, 8 action enum values including the rollback-vs-fix-and-retry split on failed-backup), the in-process TypeScript mirror (migrateOneSetV4 + dabblerSessionSets.migrateToV4 right-click command — Lightweight-tier consumer repos that never install dabbler-ai-router can migrate too), the formal rollback procedure doc (docs/v3-to-v4-rollback-procedure.md), the deferred-from-S2 needsMigration detector expansion (canonical v3 files now flag with target=4; ActionRegistry surfaces 'Migrate to v4 schema' instead of 'Migrate to v3 schema'), and a Layer-3 Playwright smoke for the v3 → v4 badge. New SessionSet.migrationTargetSchemaVersion field carries the target so the menu picks the right command. Cross-provider verification ran one round against gpt-5-4 tier 3 ($0.288 total) and returned VERIFIED with no must-fix critical items; 4 IMPORTANT items were all addressed in-flight per memory feedback_dont_hide_behind_out_of_scope (rollback-doc scope tightened; detector hoisted above normalize so a normalize failure no longer eats the badge; parity-contract docstring scope clarified; 4 additional regression tests added). Real-world dry-run against the repo's 48 historical session-sets: 47 would migrate cleanly; 1 (048-lightweight-tier-parity, an audit-stub with empty sessions[]) refused via would-violate — proving the new get_progress-validation step works as designed. All 881 Python tests pass (+31 new). All 593 TS unit tests pass (+30 new); same 2 pre-existing baseline failures unchanged. Cumulative S1+S2+S3 routed cost: $0.88022 of $10 NTE (8.8%).

reason.specifics: Set 047 Session 4 is the writer-flip phase part 1 (Python writers emit v4) per the scope-locked spec.md §4: all Python writers — register_session_start, _flip_state_to_closed, mark_session_complete, cancel_session_set — emit v4 shape. v3-shim reader stays in place; readers continue to handle both. Python test fixtures updated; v3 fixtures retained for shim coverage. Claude Opus 4.7 high-effort is the same orchestrator that closed S1, S2, and S3 and is the right pick for the cross-cutting writer touch — every Python writer site needs to drop the derived top-level fields and switch to per-session metadata writes, preserving the dual-write contract for the reader-first phase.
